name: Buttonwood Bay RV Resort
description: Buttonwood Bay RV Resort is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sebring, FL. There are 5 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to lights.

name: Charlie Brown Park
description:
Brand new courts each fenced in.  I think there are also lights for evening play.

There are six regular quarts and one singles Court

name: Genesis Center-First Presbyterian Church
description: Come play pickleball at Genesis Center-First Presbyterian Church in Lake Placid, FL! There are 3 indoor acrylic courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms and water. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are free.
open_play_schedule: Subject to change week to week

name: Highlands County Tennis Center
description: Come play pickleball at Highlands County Tennis Center in Sebring, FL! There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets.

name: Reflections
description: Reflections is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Avon Park, FL. There are 2 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts can be reserved.

name: South Florida Community College
description: South Florida Community College is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Avon Park, FL. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Amenities include lights and restrooms.

name: Spring Lake Pickleball Club
description: All 6 courts are individually fenced in.

name: Sun n Lake
description: Come play pickleball at Sun n Lake in Sebring, FL! There are 14 outdoor asphalt and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, lights, and water. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — or a $5 a day drop in fee for non members

name: Sun 'n Lakes Subdivision
description: Sun 'n Lakes Subdivision is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Lake Placid, FL. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Amenities include restrooms and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are private.

name: Tanglewood
description: Tanglewood is a place to play pickleball in Sebring, FL. There are 8 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play.

name: Thakkar Tennis and Pickleball Center
fees: Fee required — $5 daily drop in, $30 per month, $200 per year or $50 for under 25 years old, per year.

name: Twin Lakes at Avon Park 55+ Community
description:
Welcome Pickleball Players! 🏓☀️ Twin Lakes welcomes players that are 45 years of age and up, as we are a 55+ community with brand new pickleball courts! We have 4 dedicated asphalt courts with permanent lines and nets.

Our newly introduced Free Open Play Saturdays is for all skill levels and currently first come first serve. We will consider reserving the courts for serious players as we go along, but no reservations are taken at this time. 

Signing our waiver is mandatory prior to playing. Please visit the office in the clubhouse any time and we will happily get you signed up! Our office hours are Tuesday-Saturday 9:30AM-5:00PM, Sunday 11:00AM-4:30PM, and we are closed on Mondays. Please call 863-758-0159 if you should have any questions.

name: Waters Edge
description: Come play pickleball at Waters Edge in Lake Placid, FL! The court surface is asphalt. You'll need to bring your own net.
